key_evidence: Federal whistleblower suit (Gruendel, N.D. Cal.) alleges Figure had no formal safety procedures, incident-reporting, or risk-assessment processes for its robots.
Impact testing allegedly showed force more than twice that needed to fracture an adult human skull; a malfunctioning robot allegedly gashed a steel refrigerator door near an employee.
An investor-approved home-deployment safety roadmap was allegedly downgraded after the ~$39B round (flagged as potentially fraudulent); e-stop and a safety feature allegedly abandoned.
The designated head of product safety was allegedly terminated after escalating concerns; Figure denies wrongdoing and cites poor performance.

evidence.quote: no formal safety procedures, incident-reporting systems, or risk-assessment processes for the robots
evidence.claim: Figure AI lacked basic internal safety governance when the safety lead joined
evidence.source: Gizmodo (reporting on Gruendel v. Figure AI complaint)

evidence.quote: more than twice the force necessary to fracture an adult human skull
evidence.claim: Figure robots tested at force levels capable of fracturing a human skull
evidence.source: Gizmodo (reporting on Gruendel v. Figure AI complaint)

evidence.quote: Whistleblower lawsuit claims Figure AI robots have the strength to fracture skulls
evidence.claim: Independent outlet corroborates the skull-fracture force allegation and the wrongful-termination claim
